name: JellyFederation
description: Federation media sharing between Jellyfin servers
overview: Federation media sharing between Jellyfin servers

versions.changelog:
## Fixes
- Fix WebRTC answerer receive startup when SIPSorcery delivers an already-open incoming DataChannel.
- Raise SignalR hub receive size so JSON-encoded relay chunks fit under the server cap.

## Validation
- dotnet build JellyFederation.slnx
- dotnet test --solution JellyFederation.slnx
- slopwatch analyze --fail-on warning

## What's Changed

### WebRTC reliability
- Harden ICE signaling by buffering early offers and candidates until sessions and remote descriptions are ready.
- Fix WebRTC session cancellation ownership and cleanup after success, failure, cancellation, and relay fallback.
- Add sender-side DataChannel open timeout with automatic relay fallback.
- Replace ad-hoc DataChannel EOF/header magic with typed transfer frames.
- Add bounded receive queues and DataChannel send backpressure to reduce memory growth.
- Preserve relay transfer metadata and update transfer status when relay fallback is engaged.

### TURN support
- Add optional TURN server, username, and credential settings for restrictive NAT/firewall environments.

### Documentation and tests
- Add WebRTC remediation review, tracking tasks, and range/seek streaming design notes.
- Add plugin-side WebRTC component coverage for frame handling, buffering/backpressure, signaling safeguards, and TURN configuration.

## Validation

- `dotnet build JellyFederation.slnx`
- `dotnet test --solution JellyFederation.slnx`
- `slopwatch analyze --fail-on warning`

## What's Changed
* feat(webrtc): add ICE transfer and relay fallback by @lukasschachner in https://github.com/lukasschachner/JellyFederation/pull/3


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/lukasschachner/JellyFederation/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0

## What's Changed
* feat: add quic transport by @lukasschachner in https://github.com/lukasschachner/JellyFederation/pull/1
* feat: implement error and operation outcome mapping with utilities fo… by @lukasschachner in https://github.com/lukasschachner/JellyFederation/pull/2
